response: "From f(2)=0 we get 2(8)-3(4)+2a+b=0, which simplifies to 16-12+2a+b=0, so 4+2a+b=0, giving the relationship b = -2a - 4. For x=2 to be a double root, f(2)=0 and f'(2)=0 must both hold. The derivative is f'(x)=6x^2-6x+a, so f'(2)=6(4)-6(2)+a=24-12+a=12+a=0, yielding a = -12. Substituting a=-12 into the relationship b = -2a - 4 gives b = -2(-12) - 4 = 24 - 4 = 20. Thus, the values are a = -12 and b = 20."
